reasoning:

Expand Energy's stock fell roughly 6% on Feb 9 following the sudden departure of its CEO and a planned relocation to Houston. The company will report Q4 2025 earnings after the bell on Feb 17. Sudden CEO departures leading into earnings often signal underlying issues or upcoming disappointing guidance/deck-clearing by the interim team, skewing near-term risk to the downside. Furthermore, technical trend models from the yfinance tool yield a combined probability of 0.41 for exceeding the threshold. 

For the first resolution date (Feb 22), the stock will have just absorbed the Feb 17 earnings report and guidance under the interim CEO. While a negative reaction could happen, some of the bad news was already priced into the recent 6% drop, leading to an estimated 42% chance of increasing above the threshold. 

For the second resolution date (March 17), the horizon is slightly longer, bringing the baseline probability closer to an uninformed 50/50. However, the company is almost certainly expected to hit an ex-dividend date between late February and early March (based on prior quarterly schedules in mid-Feb/early March), which will mechanically reduce the stock price by the dividend amount (typically $0.58+), keeping the probability slightly below 50%. Therefore, the probability for March 17 is set at 47%.
